methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 13 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
HUMAIN is a sovereign-stage AI & Software company backed by PIF with over $40B in capitalized funding. The peer universe consists of 13 same-sector players: 1 public comparable (Palantir) and 8 private firms including Waymo, Figure AI, Applied Intuition, and Skild AI. This fragmented landscape—spanning autonomous vehicles, foundation models, robotics, and enterprise software—reflects the heterogeneity of frontier AI ventures at scale.

Peer-median valuation methodology is inapplicable here. The analysis identified zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-tier counterparts meeting minimum thresholds (n≥3). While 13 firms operate in AI & Software broadly, none cluster sufficiently with HUMAIN's sovereign-funding profile and stage. Falling back to all-sector medians would introduce material bias given the wide divergence in business models, revenue maturity, and capital deployment timelines across the peer set.

This analysis cannot support a point estimate or range without comparable transactions or financial metrics. Valuation of HUMAIN requires either (1) expansion of the peer set to adjacent mega-cap private financings outside AI & Software, (2) discounted cash flow analysis anchored to explicit revenue and margin assumptions, or (3) precedent M&A or secondary transaction data. The key uncertainty is comparability: sovereign-stage AI companies remain too few and too heterogeneous to yield reliable multiple-based benchmarks. Further diligence on HUMAIN's revenue, unit economics, and capital efficiency is essential.
